title: The One With Rachels Sister (1)

jokes.text: This coming from the man who couldn't split our $80 phone bill in half.
jokes.setup: Joey confidently calculates complex coffee orders totaling $12.75
jokes.punchline: Rachel points out Joey's inability to perform simple division ($80 ÷ 2 = $40)
jokes.explanation: Sharp character-based contradiction joke with excellent economy. The juxtaposition is perfect and very much in Rachel's voice.

dialogue.speaker: Joey
dialogue.line: Okay, so that's a venti caramel macchiato, two grande lattes, one short americano, and a tall cappuccino with an extra shot. That'll be $12.75.

dialogue.speaker: Rachel
dialogue.line: Joey, wait. If someone gives you $80 for something that costs $40, how much change do you give back?

dialogue.speaker: Rachel
dialogue.line: Right. So how did you just add up a coffee order?

jokes.text: Well, he's not used to women being so forward... but I could check with him. He says it's okay.
jokes.setup: Woman asks Joey out on a date
jokes.punchline: Joey pretends to consult with himself and immediately responds as if he's a different person
jokes.explanation: Perfectly captures Joey's opportunistic but somewhat dim nature. The instant 'consultation' is beautifully executed absurdist humor.

dialogue.speaker: Woman
dialogue.line: So, do you want to go out on a date with me?

dialogue.speaker: Joey
dialogue.line: Hold on, let me check with myself.

dialogue.speaker: Joey
dialogue.line: Yeah, he says yes.

jokes.text: It will be when you look like that in a tight skirt.
jokes.setup: Friends ask why their stuff isn't free
jokes.punchline: Joey explains his shallow but honest criteria for free coffee
jokes.explanation: Classic Joey - bluntly honest about his superficiality. Good character integration but not particularly original.

dialogue.speaker: Joey
dialogue.line: Hey, if you guys wanna come to Central Perk and get free coffee, you just gotta be really, really good looking.

dialogue.speaker: Joey
dialogue.line: That's it. That's the only qualification.

jokes.text: I'm pretty.
jokes.setup: Joey offers Monica a free scone after explaining his pretty girl policy
jokes.punchline: Monica's simple, matter-of-fact acceptance of the compliment
jokes.explanation: Perfect Monica moment - her competitive nature and need for validation expressed in two words. Maximum economy.

dialogue.speaker: Monica
dialogue.line: Oh, well, thank you.

jokes.text: Phoebe frantically searching through her purse for gum while everyone waits
jokes.setup: Someone asks for gum, Phoebe eagerly offers to help
jokes.punchline: Extended awkward search that goes on way too long until the person gives up
jokes.explanation: Classic Phoebe obliviousness played for physical comedy. The length creates the cringe but hurts economy.

dialogue.speaker: Phoebe
dialogue.line: Oh! Um, let me check. I have gum somewhere in here.

dialogue.speaker: Phoebe
dialogue.line: Okay, just... just give me a second. It's in here somewhere.

dialogue.speaker: Phoebe
dialogue.line: No, no, wait. I know I have it. Hold on.

dialogue.speaker: Phoebe
dialogue.line: It's gotta be... it's in one of these pockets...

dialogue.speaker: Phoebe
dialogue.line: Okay, this is taking longer than I thought. Just... bear with me.

dialogue.speaker: Phoebe
dialogue.line: I swear I have gum. I definitely have gum.

dialogue.speaker: Other Character
dialogue.line: You know what, it's okay. Never mind.

dialogue.speaker: Phoebe
dialogue.line: No! I got it! I— oh. Okay.

jokes.text: I don't get sick. Getting sick is for weaklings. It's for pansies.
jokes.setup: Monica claims she's not sick despite being sent home from work
jokes.punchline: Her over-the-top macho posturing while clearly ill
jokes.explanation: Good character moment showing Monica's competitive denial, but the 'pansies' language feels dated.

dialogue.speaker: Monica
dialogue.line: I'm not sick! I'm fine! I feel great!

dialogue.speaker: Monica
dialogue.line: See? I can do a pushup. I can do... okay, maybe just a half pushup.

dialogue.speaker: Monica
dialogue.line: I'm a warrior! A strong, powerful warrior who definitely doesn't have a fever and isn't sweating through my clothes right now.

jokes.text: When you put a D at the end of 'fine,' you're not fine.
jokes.setup: Monica insists she's 'fine' while clearly congested
jokes.punchline: Chandler's clever observation about her nasal pronunciation
jokes.explanation: Excellent wordplay that's both clever and observational. Very quotable and shows Chandler's wit perfectly.

dialogue.speaker: Monica
dialogue.line: I'm fine!

dialogue.speaker: Chandler
dialogue.line: You're not fine, you're 'fide'. You've said everything twice now - you've said 'I'b fine' and 'I'b great'. You sound like you're trying to speak in code.

jokes.text: You know, it's a really hard word to say.
jokes.setup: Monica tries to prove she can say 'fine' clearly
jokes.punchline: She admits defeat, acknowledging the difficulty
jokes.explanation: Nice follow-up to Chandler's line but less impactful. Monica's rare moment of admitting defeat.

dialogue.speaker: Monica
dialogue.line: Fine.

dialogue.speaker: Monica
dialogue.line: See? Fine. I said it perfectly clearly.

dialogue.speaker: Monica
dialogue.line: What? Why are you all looking at me like that?

dialogue.speaker: Monica
dialogue.line: Okay, okay, I know what you're thinking - but I CAN say 'fine' without it sounding like 'foin.'

dialogue.speaker: Monica
dialogue.line: Fine. Fine fine fine fine fine.

dialogue.speaker: Monica
dialogue.line: You know what? Forget it. This is impossible.

jokes.text: Which sister is this? Is this the spoiled one or the one that bit her?
jokes.setup: Jill arrives as Rachel's sister
jokes.punchline: Chandler's blunt categorization of Rachel's sisters by their worst traits
jokes.explanation: Perfect Chandler - inappropriately direct but hilariously accurate. Good callback to established lore.

dialogue.speaker: Chandler
dialogue.line: So you're Rachel's sister. Welcome to the group.

dialogue.speaker: Chandler
dialogue.line: You know, I've met both your sisters now. One's a spoiled brat, and the other one's a lunatic.

dialogue.speaker: Chandler
dialogue.line: I'm not saying which is which.

jokes.text: Never mind, I got it.
jokes.setup: Chandler asks which sister this is
jokes.punchline: Jill's immediate behavior (talking about being cut off) answers his question
jokes.explanation: Brilliant economy - three words that perfectly capture the reveal. Classic Chandler timing and observation.

dialogue.speaker: Chandler
dialogue.line: Which sister is this?

dialogue.speaker: Jill
dialogue.line: I've been cut off. My father won't give me any more money.

jokes.text: Boy, did we make friends with the wrong sister.
jokes.setup: Jill reveals she bought someone else a boat
jokes.punchline: Monica's realization about missed opportunities for generosity
jokes.explanation: Good observational humor that captures everyone's thoughts. Monica's practical nature shines through.

dialogue.speaker: Monica
dialogue.line: Wait, you bought someone a boat? Who buys someone a boat?

dialogue.speaker: Jill
dialogue.line: Well, I thought it would be a nice gesture.

dialogue.speaker: Monica
dialogue.line: A nice gesture? Jill, that's not a gesture, that's a down payment on a friendship! I've been buying people coffee for years. COFFEE! And here you are with a boat!

jokes.text: Aw. I like cars.
jokes.setup: Group hug after emotional moment about Jill getting her life together
jokes.punchline: Phoebe's random, simple comment completely unrelated to the moment
jokes.explanation: Pure Phoebe - completely derailing a serious moment with innocent randomness. Perfect character integration.

dialogue.speaker: Phoebe
dialogue.line: Oh! I just realized I have gum in my hair.

jokes.text: Oh. What if they came in third in a modeling contest?
jokes.setup: Manager explains they only give free food on birthdays
jokes.punchline: Joey's oddly specific hypothetical scenario for free food
jokes.explanation: Hilariously specific Joey logic. The randomness of 'third place' makes it funnier than first or second would be.

dialogue.speaker: Manager
dialogue.line: We only give free food on birthdays.

dialogue.speaker: Joey
dialogue.line: What if it's your birthday, but you're in a coma? Do you still get the free food?

jokes.text: Oh, Mr. Scientist has to get all technical.
jokes.setup: Ross points out that using dad's credit card isn't independent
jokes.punchline: Jill dismisses basic logic as being overly technical
jokes.explanation: Good character establishment for Jill - shows her privileged dismissal of reality. 'Mr. Scientist' is a nice touch.

dialogue.speaker: Ross
dialogue.line: Using Dad's credit card isn't exactly being independent.

dialogue.speaker: Jill
dialogue.line: That's just being overly technical.

jokes.text: I would love that job.
jokes.setup: Someone sarcastically asks who made Rachel queen of the world
jokes.punchline: Monica's earnest enthusiasm for the position
jokes.explanation: Perfect Monica - her controlling nature and competitiveness expressed in genuine enthusiasm for power.

dialogue.speaker: Monica
dialogue.line: Well, I did! And let me tell you, she's doing an amazing job. The healthcare system alone—

jokes.text: No, of course. Of course I've heard of them.
jokes.setup: Rachel asks if Ross has heard of 'apartment pants'
jokes.punchline: Rachel's obvious lie to avoid looking foolish about the made-up term
jokes.explanation: Decent cringe moment showing Rachel's pride, but not particularly memorable or original.

dialogue.speaker: Rachel
dialogue.line: Have you heard of apartment pants?

dialogue.speaker: Ross
dialogue.line: No.

dialogue.speaker: Rachel
dialogue.line: Oh, they're huge right now. Everyone's wearing them. I read about it in a magazine.

jokes.text: It's a rug.
jokes.setup: Rachel asks what a pashmina is
jokes.punchline: Ross's hilariously wrong but confident definition
jokes.explanation: Brilliant economy and perfect character moment. Ross's know-it-all nature backfiring spectacularly in just three words.

dialogue.speaker: Rachel
dialogue.line: What is a pashmina?

dialogue.speaker: Ross
dialogue.line: It's a type of wool. From the Himalayan mountain goat. Very soft, very expensive.

jokes.text: Oh, come on, you really think that's gonna work on me? I invented that.
jokes.setup: Jill gives Rachel puppy dog eyes as an apology
jokes.punchline: Rachel's claim to have invented the manipulative look
jokes.explanation: Good sister dynamic and character insight. Rachel's protective expertise over manipulation tactics.

dialogue.speaker: Rachel
dialogue.line: Oh please, Jill. I invented the puppy dog eyes. I perfected them. I am the master of the puppy dog eyes.

jokes.text: Yeah. Everything but the little blue one. That's the best one.
jokes.setup: Jill complains Rachel took all her stuff
jokes.punchline: Ross subtly points out Rachel kept the nicest item for herself
jokes.explanation: Nice observational moment that hints at Rachel's selfishness while maintaining plausible deniability.

dialogue.speaker: Jill
dialogue.line: Rachel took all my stuff!

dialogue.speaker: Ross
dialogue.line: Well, she did leave you the lamp.

jokes.text: Oh, my gosh, that was so lame. Like a pashmina could be a rug.
jokes.setup: Earlier Ross claimed a pashmina was a rug
jokes.punchline: Jill mocks the obviously wrong definition
jokes.explanation: Decent callback but fairly obvious. Sets up the reveal that Ross was clueless about fashion.

dialogue.speaker: Jill
dialogue.line: A rug? Ross, a pashmina is a shawl!

jokes.text: Were you this cute in high school?
jokes.setup: Ross and Jill bonding over their shared shopping embarrassment
jokes.punchline: Ross's awkward attempt at flirting
jokes.explanation: Typical awkward Ross flirting. In character but not particularly clever or memorable.

dialogue.speaker: Ross
dialogue.line: So, uh, Jill, I have to say, it's really nice to meet someone who understands the pain of public shopping humiliation.

dialogue.speaker: Jill
dialogue.line: I know, right? It's like, everyone's judging you.

dialogue.speaker: Ross
dialogue.line: Exactly! And, well, I was thinking maybe we could, you know, get some coffee sometime? Without the embarrassment?

dialogue.speaker: Jill
dialogue.line: Sure, that sounds nice.

dialogue.speaker: Ross
dialogue.line: Great! Because I find you very... very... produceable. I mean— available. I meant available.

jokes.text: I know, let's rest and drink lots of fluids.
jokes.setup: Monica suggests doing something crazy
jokes.punchline: Chandler's sarcastically practical suggestion for her illness
jokes.explanation: Good Chandler sarcasm delivered with perfect timing. The mundane suggestion as 'crazy' activity works well.

dialogue.speaker: Monica
dialogue.line: I should do something crazy!

dialogue.speaker: Chandler
dialogue.line: Okay, but maybe start with something like... see a doctor?

jokes.text: See, that would be impossible to resist if you weren't all drippy here.
jokes.setup: Monica seductively invites Chandler to bed
jokes.punchline: Chandler's honest but unromantic assessment of her congestion
jokes.explanation: Good balance of attraction and revulsion. 'Drippy here' is appropriately gross but not too crude.

dialogue.speaker: Monica
dialogue.line: Come to bed.

dialogue.speaker: Chandler
dialogue.line: Okay, but you sound like Darth Vader.

jokes.text: Yeah, I don't think you should say that even when you're healthy.
jokes.setup: Monica asks if Chandler wants to 'get with this'
jokes.punchline: Chandler's critique of her seduction technique in general
jokes.explanation: Great relationship humor - the honest critique of your partner's bad pickup lines. Very Chandler.

dialogue.speaker: Monica
dialogue.line: Do you want to get with this?

dialogue.speaker: Chandler
dialogue.line: I'm sorry, could you be any less sexy?

jokes.text: It better not be about the apartment pants... because I just pitched the idea to my boss at Ralph Lauren, and she loved it.
jokes.setup: Monica starts to tell Rachel something important
jokes.punchline: Rachel's revelation that she's turned the fake concept into a real business pitch
jokes.explanation: Brilliant escalation of the apartment pants joke. Rachel's overconfidence in fashion perfectly captured.

dialogue.speaker: Monica
dialogue.line: Rachel, I need to tell you something important.

dialogue.speaker: Rachel
dialogue.line: What? What is it?

dialogue.speaker: Monica
dialogue.line: Well, you know that thing we were just joking about?

dialogue.speaker: Rachel
dialogue.line: Yeah?

dialogue.speaker: Rachel
dialogue.line: I already pitched it to three investors and they loved it.

jokes.text: Oh, my God, and they're gonna have sex. Oh, no. What if he marries her too?
jokes.setup: Rachel learns Ross might date her sister
jokes.punchline: Rachel's spiral from dating to marriage in seconds
jokes.explanation: Good character moment showing Rachel's catastrophizing and reference to Ross's marriage history.

dialogue.speaker: Rachel
dialogue.line: He's going to marry her. I know it. He's going to marry Monica.

dialogue.speaker: Rachel
dialogue.line: They're going to get married and have kids and buy a house in Connecticut.

dialogue.speaker: Rachel
dialogue.line: And I'm going to be alone. Forever. With my cats.

dialogue.speaker: Rachel
dialogue.line: Actually, I don't even like cats.

jokes.text: But great news about the apartment pants, huh?
jokes.setup: Rachel has emotional breakdown about Ross and Jill
jokes.punchline: Monica's cheerfully inappropriate topic change to business success
jokes.explanation: Perfect comedic timing and misdirection. Monica's practical optimism cutting through Rachel's drama.

dialogue.speaker: Monica
dialogue.line: So anyway, I got that promotion at work!

jokes.text: Happy birthday to you... Happy birthday, dear Annie... Amy
jokes.setup: Customer expects free coffee for her birthday
jokes.punchline: Joey doesn't even know her name while singing to her
jokes.explanation: Good continuation of Joey's scheme. The name confusion adds authenticity to his desperation.

dialogue.speaker: Joey
dialogue.line: Happy birthday to you, happy birthday to you, happy birthday dear... uh...

dialogue.speaker: Customer
dialogue.line: It's Sarah.

dialogue.speaker: Joey
dialogue.line: Happy birthday, dear Sarah!

jokes.text: Yeah, not in here it isn't.
jokes.setup: Customer claims it's her birthday too
jokes.punchline: Gunther's authoritative shutdown of the birthday claim
jokes.explanation: Perfect Gunther line - his deadpan authority over coffee shop birthday rules. Great economy and character voice.

dialogue.speaker: Gunther
dialogue.line: It is not your birthday.

jokes.text: Are you saying he's a geek?
jokes.setup: Jill calls Ross 'bookish'
jokes.punchline: Rachel's defensive reaction despite agreeing with the assessment
jokes.explanation: Good character moment showing Rachel's protective instincts despite her own criticisms of Ross.

dialogue.speaker: Rachel
dialogue.line: Bookish? Ross is not bookish!

dialogue.speaker: Jill
dialogue.line: He has like seven books on his nightstand.

dialogue.speaker: Rachel
dialogue.line: Well, yeah, but that doesn't make him bookish!

jokes.text: What? She died, Jill.
jokes.setup: Rachel warns about a little girl who tried too much too fast
jokes.punchline: The absurdly dark conclusion to what seemed like a cautionary tale
jokes.explanation: Brilliantly dark and unexpected. The deadpan delivery of death as consequence for dating variety is absurdist perfection.

dialogue.speaker: Rachel
dialogue.line: She tried to do too much too fast, you know? One day she's learning to ride a bike, the next day she's asking for a credit card.

dialogue.speaker: Monica
dialogue.line: That's actually pretty normal for kids these days.

dialogue.speaker: Rachel
dialogue.line: No, I mean she literally died.

jokes.text: Calling Dr. Big. Dr. Big to the bed.
jokes.setup: Monica trying to seduce Chandler while sick
jokes.punchline: Her attempt at sexy medical roleplay with terrible execution
jokes.explanation: Monica's awkward attempt at being sexy. The medical theme works with her illness but execution is clunky.

dialogue.speaker: Monica
dialogue.line: Hey Chandler, I'm a nurse and you're my patient.

dialogue.speaker: Chandler
dialogue.line: Monica, you're burning up. You have a 103-degree fever.

dialogue.speaker: Monica
dialogue.line: That's right. Now take your medicine.

dialogue.speaker: Chandler
dialogue.line: The medicine is a suppository.

dialogue.speaker: Monica
dialogue.line: Even better.

dialogue.speaker: Chandler
dialogue.line: Monica, you just threw up on my shoes.

dialogue.speaker: Monica
dialogue.line: That's... part of the fantasy.

jokes.text: Come on, get into bed. I wanna prove that I'm not sick. I wanna make you feel as good as I feel.
jokes.setup: Monica insists she's not sick
jokes.punchline: She wants Chandler to feel as terrible as she obviously does
jokes.explanation: Good ironic humor - Monica's unintentionally threatening offer of shared misery while denying illness.

dialogue.speaker: Monica
dialogue.line: I'm not sick.

dialogue.speaker: Chandler
dialogue.line: Monica, you sound like a dying moose.

dialogue.speaker: Monica
dialogue.line: I said I'm not sick!

dialogue.speaker: Chandler
dialogue.line: Okay, okay.

dialogue.speaker: Monica
dialogue.line: But you're going to be.

jokes.text: You've sung 'Happy Birthday' to 20 different women today.
jokes.setup: Joey protests about paying for a birthday customer's muffin
jokes.punchline: Gunther's specific count of Joey's scam attempts
jokes.explanation: Good escalation showing the scope of Joey's scheme. Gunther's tracking adds credibility to the shutdown.

dialogue.speaker: Joey
dialogue.line: Why should I pay for her muffin? She comes in here all the time!

dialogue.speaker: Gunther
dialogue.line: Because that's how a business works, Joey. And by the way, this is the 47th time you've tried this.

jokes.text: I think she's cute... but I never would've thought of going out with her. Never.
jokes.setup: Ross talks to Rachel about dating Jill
jokes.punchline: His emphatic 'never' while clearly having thought about it
jokes.explanation: Ross's transparent denial. The repetition of 'never' makes his interest obvious.

dialogue.speaker: Rachel
dialogue.line: So are you gonna ask Jill out?

dialogue.speaker: Ross
dialogue.line: Never!

jokes.text: Hey, if you ever wanna go out with Monica, you have my blessing. And mine.
jokes.setup: Ross wishes he had a brother to offer Rachel in return
jokes.punchline: Monica immediately endorsing her own availability
jokes.explanation: Perfect Monica moment - her eagerness for romantic opportunity overriding any awkwardness about the situation.

dialogue.speaker: Ross
dialogue.line: I wish I had a brother to offer you, Rachel.

dialogue.speaker: Monica
dialogue.line: What about me? I'm available!

jokes.text: No, no, no. You are not getting me this way.
jokes.setup: Monica asks Chandler to rub medicine on her chest
jokes.punchline: Chandler recognizes the seduction attempt and resists
jokes.explanation: Decent relationship dynamics but fairly predictable. Chandler's wariness is in character.

dialogue.speaker: Monica
dialogue.line: Can you rub this medicine on my chest?

dialogue.speaker: Chandler
dialogue.line: I know what you're doing.

dialogue.speaker: Monica
dialogue.line: What? I just need help reaching.

dialogue.speaker: Chandler
dialogue.line: Yeah, and I'm not falling for it. I know this is a seduction attempt.

dialogue.speaker: Monica
dialogue.line: Chandler, it's medicine!

dialogue.speaker: Chandler
dialogue.line: Could I BE any more aware of what's happening here?

jokes.text: Are you kidding me? Is this turning you on?
jokes.setup: Monica rubs VapoRub on herself while Chandler watches
jokes.punchline: Monica's shocked realization that her self-medication is arousing
jokes.explanation: Funny subversion of expectations. Monica's innocuous self-care becoming accidentally seductive.

dialogue.speaker: Monica
dialogue.line: What? It's just VapoRub!

dialogue.speaker: Chandler
dialogue.line: I know, but... you're doing it wrong.

dialogue.speaker: Monica
dialogue.line: There's a wrong way to use VapoRub?

dialogue.speaker: Chandler
dialogue.line: Not for medical purposes, no.

dialogue.speaker: Monica
dialogue.line: Oh my God!

jokes.text: What about your rule about never sleeping with sick people?
jokes.setup: Chandler now wants to have sex after being turned on
jokes.punchline: Monica throws his earlier objection back at him
jokes.explanation: Good callback and role reversal. Monica getting to be the rejecting party for once.

dialogue.speaker: Monica
dialogue.line: Could I BE any more in the mood?

dialogue.speaker: Chandler
dialogue.line: Yes, actually. Could you BE any more in the mood?

dialogue.speaker: Monica
dialogue.line: Oh, so now you want to?

dialogue.speaker: Chandler
dialogue.line: Well, yeah!

dialogue.speaker: Monica
dialogue.line: I thought you had a headache.

jokes.text: Worked like a charm.
jokes.setup: Monica appears to give in to Chandler's advances
jokes.punchline: Reveals she was manipulating him the whole time
jokes.explanation: Excellent Monica manipulation reveal. Perfect economy and shows her strategic mind even while sick.

dialogue.speaker: Monica
dialogue.line: You know what? You're right. I do care about you. Maybe we should just... see what happens.

dialogue.speaker: Chandler
dialogue.line: Really?

dialogue.speaker: Monica
dialogue.line: No! I was just seeing how long it would take before you completely fell apart. About three seconds. New personal record.

jokes.text: It's kind of slutty. It's yours.
jokes.setup: Rachel criticizes Jill's dress as slutty
jokes.punchline: Jill reveals it belongs to Rachel
jokes.explanation: Perfect setup and reveal. Rachel's hypocrisy exposed with excellent economy and timing.

dialogue.speaker: Rachel
dialogue.line: That dress is so slutty.

dialogue.speaker: Jill
dialogue.line: It's yours.

jokes.text: Yeah, well, I'm a slut. Me too.
jokes.setup: Discussion about slutty clothing
jokes.punchline: Both sisters casually accepting and embracing the label
jokes.explanation: Decent escalation of the slut comment. Shows the sisters' casual attitude toward judgment.

dialogue.speaker: Jill
dialogue.line: So you're a slut.

dialogue.speaker: Rachel
dialogue.line: Yeah, I guess I am.

dialogue.speaker: Jill
dialogue.line: Well, so am I.

jokes.text: Uh, I'm just looking out your window at the view.
jokes.setup: Monica finds Rachel inexplicably in her apartment
jokes.punchline: Rachel's transparently false excuse while obviously spying
jokes.explanation: Good cringe moment showing Rachel's terrible lying while stalking her sister's date.

dialogue.speaker: Monica
dialogue.line: Rachel, what are you doing in my apartment?

dialogue.speaker: Rachel
dialogue.line: Oh, hi! I was just... looking for my earring. I think I left it here.

dialogue.speaker: Monica
dialogue.line: Your earring? Rachel, you're standing in front of my bedroom window with binoculars.

dialogue.speaker: Rachel
dialogue.line: Right, yes. Well, the earring is very small, and I have poor vision.

jokes.text: Ugh. She is a slut.
jokes.setup: Rachel sees what she interprets as her sister being promiscuous
jokes.punchline: Rachel's hypocritical judgment after calling herself a slut earlier
jokes.explanation: Shows Rachel's hypocrisy but not particularly clever. The callback works but lacks impact.

dialogue.speaker: Rachel
dialogue.line: Oh my God, Melissa, what are you doing? You can't just sleep with every guy you meet!

dialogue.speaker: Melissa
dialogue.line: Says the girl who told me she's slept with like half of Manhattan.

dialogue.speaker: Rachel
dialogue.line: That's different! I'm a slut by choice, not by... by accident!

jokes.text: Hoo-hoo. He's gonna get some.
jokes.setup: Watching Ross and Jill through the window
jokes.punchline: Monica's gleeful, crude observation about impending sex
jokes.explanation: Monica's inappropriate enthusiasm. In character but not particularly clever or memorable.

dialogue.speaker: Monica
dialogue.line: Oh my God, she's going back to his apartment!

dialogue.speaker: Monica
dialogue.line: She's going back to his apartment!

jokes.text: Of the glare... from the streetlight out of his apartment. You know, so, um, he's closed the drapes there... so he can have a nice pleasant conversation with your little sister.
jokes.setup: Monica's crude comment about Ross getting some
jokes.punchline: Chandler's elaborate euphemistic explanation for closing curtains
jokes.explanation: Classic Chandler - over-elaborate politeness to avoid crude reality. Good character voice and structure.

dialogue.speaker: Chandler
dialogue.line: Could I BE any more of a visual aid for the concept of privacy? I'm closing these curtains not because I'm shy, but because I respect the sanctity of human connection and the mutual agreement between two consenting adults that certain moments deserve a little... atmospheric enhancement. In other words, I'm helping Ross create an environment conducive to romance, which involves blocking out the harsh realities of the outside world, namely the pizza guy who always shows up at exactly the wrong time.

tmdb_overview: Joey gets in trouble for giving attractive female customers free food at Central Perk. A sick Monica desperately tries to convince Chandler that she's not sick. After her father cuts her off, Rachel's sister Jill comes to town. Rachel encourages her to start an independent life by finding a job and an apartment, but one thing leads to another and Jill ends up asking Ross out on a date. Even though Rachel doesn't want to admit it, she clearly isn't okay with that...
insight: Sister visit delivers steady character work, but 75-point score reflects lighter setup episode before conflict.
